untranslatable_reason: This signal requires calendar-based seasonality detection (specific months of the year: the favorable 6-month period vs. summer doldrums/unfavorable 6-month period). The allowed indicators list contains only technical price/volume indicators and does not include a calendar month or seasonality indicator. The rule cannot be expressed using only the provided indicators (price, moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger Bands, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, Pivots, Volume ratios) without adding a calendar/date-based indicator.
translation_notes: Signal originates from Yale Hirsch's discovery of seasonal patterns (Buy and Hold Strategy). Entry should occur at the start of the favorable 6-month period (historically November-April in US stock market), and exit at the end (approximately April). This requires external calendar logic outside the scope of technical indicators. To implement this signal, the backtest engine would need to add a calendar indicator or month-of-year condition handler.
